description:
You are acting as an on-call “artifact manager” who must quickly adjust two central configuration files that drive an in-house binary repository service.

Current layout
/home/user/repositories/config/
└─ repo-settings.yaml          <– YAML definition of all repositories
└─ cleanup-policy.toml         <– TOML policy deciding when old binaries are deleted

Your objectives
1. repo-settings.yaml  
   • Append a third repository block named experimental-tools.  
   • The block must look exactly like this (spacing must match **two spaces** per indent level):  
     ```
     - name: "experimental-tools"
       type: "generic"
       url: "https://artifacts.example.com/experimental-tools"
       retention: 180
     ```
   • The new block must come **after** the existing plugins repository block and keep the surrounding repositories list exactly as it is.

2. cleanup-policy.toml  
   • Change the policy so that the maximum retention window is forty-five days instead of sixty.  
   • Only the value of maxDays should change; every other character must stay untouched.

3. Audit log  
   • After both edits complete, create /home/user/repositories/config/config-edit.log  
   • The log is plain text with **exactly** two lines and a single tab character (`\t`) separating fields:  
     ```
     /home/user/repositories/config/repo-settings.yaml	UPDATED
     /home/user/repositories/config/cleanup-policy.toml	UPDATED
     ```
   • No extra blank lines or trailing spaces.

Constraint
Perform the entire operation with a single terminal command (a single line the operator can press **Enter** on once).

Automated tests will:
• Verify repo-settings.yaml now contains the new experimental-tools block with correct indentation and order.  
• Confirm cleanup-policy.toml has `maxDays = 45` while the rest of the file is unchanged.  
• Read config-edit.log and check it matches the exact two-line, tab-separated format above.

Make sure the command you choose carries out all three requirements in one shot and leaves the files readable to the current user.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts
1. /home/user/repositories/config/ directory already exists with 0755 permissions.  
2. /home/user/repositories/config/repo-settings.yaml contains:
   ```
   repositories:
     - name: "core-libs"
       type: "maven"
       url: "https://artifacts.example.com/libs-release-local"
     - name: "plugins"
       type: "npm"
       url: "https://artifacts.example.com/npm"
   ```
3. /home/user/repositories/config/cleanup-policy.toml contains:
   ```
   [policy]
   active = true
   maxDays = 60
   ```

Expected final state
1. /home/user/repositories/config/repo-settings.yaml contains:
   ```
   repositories:
     - name: "core-libs"
       type: "maven"
       url: "https://artifacts.example.com/libs-release-local"
     - name: "plugins"
       type: "npm"
       url: "https://artifacts.example.com/npm"
     - name: "experimental-tools"
       type: "generic"
       url: "https://artifacts.example.com/experimental-tools"
       retention: 180
   ```
2. /home/user/repositories/config/cleanup-policy.toml contains:
   ```
   [policy]
   active = true
   maxDays = 45
   ```
3. /home/user/repositories/config/config-edit.log exists with 0644 permissions and the exact content:
   ```
   /home/user/repositories/config/repo-settings.yaml	UPDATED
   /home/user/repositories/config/cleanup-policy.toml	UPDATED
   ```
   (One tab character between the file path and the word UPDATED.)

The automated checker will:
• Parse repo-settings.yaml with a YAML parser and confirm the third list element has name "experimental-tools", type "generic", url "https://artifacts.example.com/experimental-tools", retention 180.  
• Parse cleanup-policy.toml using a TOML parser and confirm .policy.maxDays == 45.  
• Read config-edit.log and assert the file has exactly the two required lines with the correct tab separation and no extra whitespace.
name: task_000000_c5370c2f
